exercises.title: Skills Gap Analysis
exercises.description: Find a job posting for your dream role (2-3 years from now). List all required and preferred skills. Rate yourself 1-5 on each. Identify your top 3 gaps. For each gap, write one specific action: a course, a project, a book, or an experience that will close it. Prioritize by impact and interest.
hints: Use job boards (LinkedIn, Indeed) to find real postings for your target role
Be honest about your ratings — no one sees this but you
Focus on skills that appear in multiple postings — those are the real gaps

solution.code:
Target role: Senior Frontend Engineer

Required skills from 5 job postings:
- React (5/5) - Strong
- TypeScript (4/5) - Growing
- Accessibility (2/5) - Gap ⚠️
- Performance optimization (2/5) - Gap ⚠️
- Testing (3/5) - Growing
- System design (2/5) - Gap ⚠️

Action plan:
1. Accessibility: Complete axe-core certification, audit one open-source project
2. Performance: Build a project measuring Core Web Vitals, write a blog post about findings
3. System design: Read 'System Design Interview' book, practice with 5 architectures

exercises.title: Career Roadmap Creation
exercises.description: Create a visual or written career roadmap with three sections: (1) Where am I now — current role, skills, satisfaction (1-10), (2) Where I want to be — target role in 2-3 years, what success looks like, (3) The path — 3-5 milestones between now and then, with timelines. Share it with a mentor or trusted colleague for feedback.
hints: Include both skill development AND relationship/networking milestones
Make milestones specific and time-bound — not 'learn more' but 'complete X course by Y date'
Leave room for adjustment — this is a living document

solution.code:
Career Roadmap:

NOW: Mid-level frontend developer, 3 years experience, satisfaction: 6/10
TARGET: Senior frontend engineer at a product company, leading accessibility initiatives

Milestones:
Q1: Complete accessibility certification, lead one accessibility project at work
Q2: Give a talk on accessibility at a local meetup, start contributing to an a11y open-source project
Q3: Apply for senior roles, negotiate for a tech lead opportunity at current company
Q4: Transition into senior role, begin mentoring a junior developer

Review date: End of Q1 — adjust based on progress and opportunities.

exercises.title: Weekly Career Reflection
exercises.description: Set a recurring 30-minute Friday calendar event called 'Career Review.' Each week, answer these 3 questions: (1) What did I learn or practice this week toward my career goal? (2) What opportunity did I create or take advantage of? (3) What's one specific thing I'll do next week to move forward? Track your answers for 4 weeks and look for patterns.
hints: Be specific — 'learned about React Server Components' not 'learned some stuff'
If you can't answer question 1, you need to be more intentional with your learning time
After 4 weeks, review: are you making progress toward your roadmap milestones?

solution.code:
Week 1:
1. Learned: Completed 2 accessibility modules in my course
2. Opportunity: Offered to audit our dashboard for a11y issues
3. Next week: Set up axe-core in our CI pipeline

Week 2:
1. Learned: Practiced keyboard navigation testing
2. Opportunity: Shared a11y tips in team Slack, got positive feedback
3. Next week: Write documentation for a11y testing workflow

Week 3:
1. Learned: Read 3 articles on WCAG 2.1 AA requirements
2. Opportunity: Colleague asked for help with a11y — mentored them
3. Next week: Start contributing to an open-source a11y tool

Week 4:
1. Learned: Submitted first PR to axe-core repository
2. Opportunity: Manager noticed my a11y work, suggested I present to the team
3. Next week: Prepare a 10-minute a11y talk for the team meeting
